mission.title: cert-manager: Add support for passwordless pkcs12 keystore
mission.description: Add support for passwordless pkcs12 keystore. Requested by 34+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
I would like to push cert manager generated certificate to azure keyvault using external secrets operator. We need to use the certificate in a Azure Application Gateway and it only accepts pkcs12 format.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for passwordless pkcs12 keystore
steps.description:
### Pull Request Motivation

The fact of encrypting keystores with a password in Java keytool predates the Kubernetes era.
Since the generated keystore is stored in a Kubernetes secret, the fact of encrypting it has become irrelevant (if you can access secrets, then you can also access the secret
```yaml
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
spec:
  secretName: certificate
  keystores:
    pkcs12:
      create: true
      password: "changeit"            # This is the new field.
    jks:
      create: true
      password: "changeit"            # This is the other new field.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for passwordless pkcs12 keystore" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Pull Request Motivation

The fact of encrypting keystores with a password in Java keytool predates the Kubernetes era.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
spec:
  secretName: certificate
  keystores:
    pkcs12:
      create: true
      password: <REDACTED>"            # This is the new field.
    jks:
      create: true
      password: <REDACTED>"            # This is the other new field.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
